config.general_notes: This table tracks radiopharmaceutical products (diagnostic, therapeutic and theranostic) in development that could compete with Ratio Therapeutics over the next 1â€“5 years. The list focuses on agents in pre-registration through Phase 1 and likely to reach market in 2024-2030. Your key goal is to monitor this competitive landscape. Please use the most recent information available from public sources, including clinicaltrials.gov, company press releases, and news articles.

validation_targets.description: Official code or INN of the radiopharmaceutical
Lead company (add parent in parentheses if acquired)
Molecular target or receptor the agent binds
validation_targets.notes: Use gene/protein symbol or common receptor acronym

validation_targets.description: Main disease(s) or tumour type(s) the product is being developed for
validation_targets.notes: Concise but specific (e.g., include biomarker status)

validation_targets.description: Radionuclide used for therapy (if applicable) for this product
Radionuclide used for imaging (if theranostic or diagnostic-only) for this product

validation_targets.notes: Include molecule class if helpful (peptide, mAb, small molecule)
If multiple, list the most advanced
Infer from typical timelines if not public

validation_targets.description: Notable regulatory designations (BTD, Fast Track, Orphan-Drug)
validation_targets.notes: List agency and year; '-' if none
Include major CDMO / isotope-supply partners if relevant

validation_targets.description: Latest important news on the product development (includes date of information release)
validation_targets.notes:
Bullets with aggregated news on a product. Confidence is only assesed for new news that is to be added - all other data is past forward. Include past news and append new news to the beginning of the list. Starts with a dash, date: news (confidence level). Use 
 to separate each line.
examples:
- 4/10/2025: BLA resubmission in Q1 2025 after OS update requested (High Confidence)
- 3/10/2025: Phase 3 trial results expected in Q2 2025 (High Confidence)
- 2/10/2025: Phase 3 trial initiated (High Confidence)
- 1/10/2025: Phase 2 trial results expected in Q4 2025 (High Confidence)

rows.Indication: Solid tumors (including sarcoma, gastrointestinal tract cancer, pancreatic, breast, and colorectal cancers; FAP-expressing cancers)
rows.Recent News:
- 03/12/2024: Ratio announces expansion of manufacturing agreement with PharmaLogic for FAP-targeted radiopharmaceuticals (High Confidence)
- 07/11/2023: First patient dosed in Phase I study evaluating a novel FAP-targeted radiopharmaceutical for PET imaging (High Confidence)
- 02/10/2024: IND cleared by FDA (High Confidence)

Indication.value: Solid tumors (including sarcoma, gastrointestinal tract cancer, pancreatic, breast, and colorectal cancers; FAP-expressing cancers)
Indication.quote: A Phase 1/2a Study Utilizing 64Cu-LNTH-1363S (64Cu Radiolabeled FAPi PET/CT Imaging Agent) in Patients with Sarcoma or Gastrointestinal Tract Cancer... Cancer-associated fibroblasts selectively expressing FAP comprise up to 90 percent of the tumor mass in highly desmoplastic cancers including pancreatic, breast, and colorectal.

Therapeutic Radionuclide.quote: Ratio’s clinical candidate, RTX-1363S, is a highly selective, high affinity FAP inhibitor that will be radiolabeled with copper-64 (Cu-64), a positron-emitting radionuclide with a half-life of 12.7 hr... Results from the study will inform further development of [Cu-64]-labeled RTX-1363S as a FAP-targeted tumor imaging agent.

Diagnostic Radionuclide.quote: Ratio’s clinical candidate, RTX-1363S, is a highly selective, high affinity FAP inhibitor that will be radiolabeled with copper-64 (Cu-64), a positron-emitting radionuclide with a half-life of 12.7 hr.

Modality Type.quote: Ratio’s clinical candidate, RTX-1363S, is a highly selective, high affinity FAP inhibitor that will be radiolabeled with copper-64 (Cu-64)... Results from the study will inform further development of [Cu-64]-labeled RTX-1363S as a FAP-targeted tumor imaging agent.

Development Stage.quote: Ratio Therapeutics on Wednesday said that it began a Phase I trial of the fibroblast activation protein-alpha (FAP)-targeted radiopharmaceutical Cu-64-labeled RTX-1363S in healthy volunteers.

Key Trial ID.quote: No direct quote available, but NCT05760989 is the only publicly listed clinical trial for [Cu-64]-labeled RTX-1363S on clinicaltrials.gov as of the latest available information.

Projected Launch.quote: No direct quote available for launch year. Typical timelines for Phase 1 radiopharmaceuticals suggest a possible launch in 2026 if development proceeds without delays, but this is not explicitly stated in available sources.

FDA-EMA Designation.quote: No public record of FDA or EMA regulatory designations (BTD, Fast Track, Orphan-Drug) for [^64Cu]RTX-1363S.

Strategic Partners.quote: Ratio, in collaboration with Lantheus and PharmaLogic, is currently conducting a Phase I trial evaluating the efficacy of a novel FAP-targeted radiopharmaceutical.

Recent News.value:
- 03/12/2024: Ratio announces expansion of manufacturing agreement with PharmaLogic for FAP-targeted radiopharmaceuticals (High Confidence)
- 07/11/2023: First patient dosed in Phase I study evaluating a novel FAP-targeted radiopharmaceutical for PET imaging (High Confidence)
- 02/10/2024: IND cleared by FDA (High Confidence)
Recent News.quote: Today marks a major milestone for Ratio as we take a crucial step forward in the development of our FAP-targeted imaging agent... RTX-1363S has the potential to transform the way we detect and monitor various epithelial-derived cancers.
